headlines.title: Mum rescued from Venezuela rubble with newborn baby tells BBC how he helped her survive
headlines.description: The "miracle" rescue of 18-day-old baby Juan David is a symbol of hope in Venezuela after the devastating earthquakes.

headlines.title: The Good Life actress Dame Penelope Keith dies aged 86
headlines.description: Tributes are paid to the "comic genius", who starred in some of Britain's most popular sitcoms.

headlines.title: Five dead following shooting in Stade, northern Germany
headlines.description: Two people have been arrested, police said.

headlines.title: One in 10 children in England referred for mental healthcare - with anxiety the main reason
headlines.description: Demand is soaring beyond capacity, meaning children in England wait years for help with various conditions.

headlines.title: Temperatures to drop this week but relief from heat may be short-lived
headlines.description: Many will experience cooler air and some rain this week before a possible return to hot weather at the weekend.

headlines.title: Navy to build drone-equipped warships instead of replacing ageing destroyers
headlines.description: The government says it will invest in modern drone-equipped vessels in the upcoming defence investment plan.

headlines.title: Michael Jackson movie becomes highest-grossing biopic of all time
headlines.description: The Michael Jackson movie was questioned by critics but has been a hit with fans around the world.

headlines.title: How population decline is exposing Germany's old divides
headlines.description: Decades after the fall of the Berlin Wall, a population slump in the east lays bare the existing divisions

headlines.title: Fitness brand founder and Olympian Les Mills dies aged 91
headlines.description: The Les Mills International brand is now known globally for choreographed workouts set to music, like Body Pump and Body Combat.

headlines.title: How the new Brazil is taking shape and why Cunha is key
headlines.description: Former Brazil midfielder Lucas Leiva explains why Manchester United striker Matheus Cunha is so important to Brazil's adaptable system under Carlo Ancelotti.

headlines.title: 'Worth every penny', say England fans at World Cup
headlines.description: As football fever sweeps the nation, England fans from Yorkshire share their World Cup experiences.

headlines.title: Clarke says standing down 'easy' decision after World Cup exit
headlines.description: Steve Clarke says it was an easy decision to step down as Scotland head coach because he always planned to depart if they did not qualify for the knock-out stages.

headlines.title: Dallas Cowboys Cheerleaders on fame, pressure and World Cup fever
headlines.description: As the Netflix show following their lives returns, the squad open up about their new-found fame.

headlines.title: How formidable sitcom star Penelope Keith won the nation's hearts
headlines.description: The life of the much-loved comedy actress best known as Margo Leadbeatter in The Good Life.

headlines.title: 'I see tourists pee in front of my house': The campervan problem on the Isle of Skye
headlines.description: The Isle of Skye is among the Highlands' most popular destinations for thousands of holidaymakers.

headlines.title: Got the tennis bug? How to play sport without paying
headlines.description: As the world's best players begin play at Wimbledon, how can you get into sport on a budget.

headlines.title: Why is pregnancy sickness drug not easily accessible to all?
headlines.description: BBC journalist Linzi Kinghorn finds out why a pregnancy sickness drug is not always accessible on the NHS.

headlines.title: Catherine completes Three Peaks Challenge to 'explore life beyond' cancer diagnosis
headlines.description: The Princess of Wales did the endurance event to raise awareness about "holistic healthcare" for cancer patients.

headlines.title: White working-class children failed by education system, says inquiry
headlines.description: The inquiry spoke to thousands of young people and their parents, as well as hundreds of teachers.

headlines.title: British American Tobacco to cut 9,000 jobs
headlines.description: The company had said earlier this year it was planning savings to make it  "more digital and AI-focused".

headlines.title: Fife man dies after being shot 'multiple times' in Caribbean
headlines.description: Daniel Vettrino, 37, was killed following the incident on in the island of Canouan in St Vincent and the Grenadines.

headlines.title: Metallica donates £20,000 to city foodbank with 'empty shelves' after sell-out gig
headlines.description: Heavy metal legends Metallica have donated £20,000 to Cardiff food bank which is enough for 9000 meals through the charity.

headlines.title: US says it has agreed to 'stand down' after exchange of strikes with Iran
headlines.description: A series of strikes over the weekend saw the US and Iran accuse each other of violating the ceasefire agreement.

headlines.title: First dinosaur bone from Antarctica found in a drawer
headlines.description: The fossil, collected in Antarctica in 1985, is part of the tail of a beast called a Titanosaur.

headlines.title: BBC News app
headlines.description: Top stories, breaking news, live reporting, and follow news topics that match your interests

headlines.title: Newscast
headlines.description: We look at new details from the controversial defence plan a new PM would inherit.

headlines.title: Test Match Special Podcast
headlines.description: Ben Stokes speaks after the announcement he's to retire from international cricket.

headlines.title: Biopic starring Will Smith as Venus and Serena Williams' father
headlines.description: Richard Williams is determined to write daughters Venus and Serena into sporting history.

headlines.title: More change needed after Stokes retires - Vaughan
headlines.description: Former captain Michael Vaughan says he will be "absolutely staggered" if there is not more leadership change in English cricket after Ben Stokes' retirement.

headlines.title: Tuchel still searching for answers out wide - Shearer
headlines.description: Former England captain Alan Shearer explains why there are lots of reasons to be positive about England so far at this World Cup, but having a settled side is not one of them.

headlines.title: Why Netherlands v Morocco is more than just a match
headlines.description: Morocco’s World Cup meeting with the Netherlands tells a story about migration, identity and the battle for Dutch-born talent.

headlines.title: I'd ticked all the boxes so decision to resign easy - Clarke
headlines.description: Steve Clarke says it was an easy decision to step down as Scotland head coach because he always planned to depart if they did not qualify for the knock-out stages.

headlines.title: Why 'big brother' Van Dijk is key to Dutch World Cup dreams
headlines.description: France's World Cup winner Olivier Giroud explains why Netherlands defender Virgil van Dijk is still the main man for his national team.
